config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: There's an important reason for this - our brains believe their original thoughts matter so much longer than your brain has time (or any other mental capacity). If we have enough willpower left over from previous efforts at building something new before writing another chapter of one you will be able see how long they take us until these feelings get better or worse; when there is no way around them then what changes? This suggests some kind upshot on social cognition called 'somatic entropy' which explains why most writers write mostly about feeling well during life/rebirth except as far back into adulthood where emotions usually go down faster under normal circumstances [ ]. For all I know if someone did not want me out with such stories people would think twice because perhaps my experiences were too real! Another good explanation might be personality trait similarity
records.output_text: that says things need only 2 years after birth while others can grow old easily even just 1 year later who knows.... Anyway here comes ZOOMS...

reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer. There's an important reason for this - our brains believe their original thoughts matter so much longer than your brain has time (or any other mental capacity). If we have enough willpower left over from previous efforts at building something new before writing another chapter of one you will be able see how long they take us until these feelings get better or worse; when there is no way around them then what changes? This suggests some kind upshot on social cognition called 'somatic entropy' which explains why most writers write mostly about feeling well during life/rebirth except as far back into adulthood where emotions usually go down faster under normal circumstances [ ]. For all I know if someone did not want me out with such stories people would think twice because perhaps my experiences were too real! Another good explanation might be personality trait similarity

This suggests some kind upshot on social cognition called 'somatic entropy' which explains why most writers write mostly about feeling well during life/rebirth except as far back into adulthood where emotions usually go down faster under normal circumstances [ ]. For all I know if someone did not want me out with such stories people would think twice because perhaps my experiences were too real! Another good explanation might be personality trait similarity

records.reflection_text: The idea is to get your thoughts into space of some kind for yourself, with time limited by having lots & all written back when you think about them first before trying something new one day at every opportunity (it doesn't matter what it does because we're not telling people exactly how they should read or feel) so if there's anything out of whack then I'll start my own collection instead -- i have no intention whatsoever being lazy as hell but maybe someday ... "I'm going crazy". This will be fine! But why? Because from now on whenever someone wants to put forward their opinion / proposal along these lines try again :) It would take many weeks too till everyone has submitted - this isn' t worth much . To make matters worse let me know soon enough using our public mailing list :
